commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: ignore RT scheduling integration tests on macOS CI

macOS CI runners (like Windows) lack RT scheduling capabilities,
causing FFB jitter, HID latency, missed ticks, fault injection,
acceptance, and benchmark tests to fail with histogram overflow
or timing violations.

Add #[cfg_attr(target_os = "macos", ignore = ...)] to all 7 tests
that already have Windows ignores for the same reason.

commit.message:
Feature/rest encounter and refactors (#21)

* Refactor resolve_ore_play to use deck_play_random

Replaces inline logic for picking a random ore card from hand and
moving it to discard with the generic deck_play_random function,
matching how combat and fishing disciplines handle their plays.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Extend HasDeckCounts trait with individual accessor methods

Change HasDeckCounts from returning &DeckCounts/&mut DeckCounts to
individual accessor methods (deck_count, hand_count, discard_count
and their _mut variants). This allows both CardCounts and DeckCounts
to implement the trait with flat fields.

Implement HasDeckCounts for: DeckCounts, CardCounts, OreCard,
FishCard, PlantCard, EnemyCardDef, and LibraryCard. This enables
player-owned cards to use the same generic deck operations
(deck_draw_random, deck_shuffle_hand, deck_play_random) as
encounter-internal cards.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Add rest encounter type (roadmap 9.4)

Implement the rest encounter discipline with:
- Rest card types, definitions, and encounter state in types.rs
- Rest discipline module with card registration (3 templates -> 5 cards x 5 copies)
- start_rest_encounter and resolve_rest_card_choice on GameState
- Action handler integration for picking and playing rest encounters
- Rest encounters compose ~20% of encounter deck (hand: 4)
- Recovery effects apply stamina/health with caps, costs in fish/herbs

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: lock IBM QPU path to 6 qubits (1 codon)

4 qubits is architecturally impossible — 2 bits/nt × 3 nt = 6 qubits minimum
per codon. Multi-codon IBM circuits (12/18 qubits) are unreliable on noisy
free-tier QPU hardware.

- backend/models/schemas.py: BioIBMSubmitRequest n_codons le=3 → le=1
- frontend/src/components/GroverPOC.jsx: IBM submit hardcodes n_codons: 1

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: IBM QPU threshold-based detection — fixes NOT_FOUND on noisy 6-qubit hardware

Root cause: detection used strict equality (max(counts) == target_bits) which fails
on noisy real QPU hardware where gate errors shift the maximum result.

Changes:
- backend/routers/quantum.py: replace strict equality with IBM_DETECT_THRESHOLD=0.05
  (5% ≈ 3.2× uniform for 6 qubits). Fix field name user_email→email in find_one/
  update_one to match insert schema. Return null confidence when no DB record so
  frontend fallback activates correctly.
- frontend/src/components/GroverPOC.jsx: fix prev-out-of-scope ReferenceError
  (prev→ibmResult outside setIbmResult callback). Use backend detection_result/
  confidence when available; threshold fallback from counts otherwise.

commit.message:
feat: capability badges in model selectors + text-based tool call interception (#133)

* feat: intercept text-based tool calls from small models

Small models (1-8B) often emit tool calls as plain JSON text instead of
using the structured tool calling protocol. Detect this pattern, parse
the JSON, resolve the kernel function, invoke it, and re-prompt the
model with the result so it produces a natural-language response.

Handles both non-streaming and streaming paths. Gracefully ignores
non-tool JSON or parse failures.

core: wire TransferJournal into download/upload for automatic resume (#519)

Integrate TransferJournal into DeviceActor transfer paths with four
key fixes for resume reliability:

- Preserve temp files on read failure when journal is active, enabling
  resume from the last flushed offset instead of starting over
- Add ETag validation (file-size check) before resuming, detecting
  server-side changes that would corrupt a resumed transfer
- Use actual file size on disk as resume offset instead of journal's
  committedBytes, which may be stale if interrupted mid-flush
- Check device operationsSupported for GetPartialObject (0x95C1) in
  addition to quirks policy flags for partial resume capability

Add MTPError.etagMismatch with full error messaging chain
(diagnostic, actionable, CLI). Add 10 tests covering ETag validation,
temp file preservation, progress tracking, and edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
